original_abstract: We report the solar-driven production of hydrogen peroxide (H₂O₂) as a clean fuel through the photocatalytic reduction of dioxygen coupled with water oxidation in acidic aqueous media. Using [RuII(Me2phen)3]2+ as a visible-light-active photocatalyst and Ir(OH)₃ as a water oxidation catalyst, H₂O₂ was successfully synthesized in O₂-saturated sulfuric acid solutions. Mechanistically, photoinduced electron transfer from the excited state of the ruthenium complex to O₂ generates [RuIII(Me2phen)3]3+ and a superoxide radical anion (O₂˙⁻). Under acidic conditions, O₂˙⁻ undergoes protonation and subsequent disproportionation to yield H₂O₂, a process that effectively competes with back electron transfer. The resulting ruthenium(III) species then oxidizes water back to O₂, facilitated by the Ir(OH)₃ nanoparticles. We further enhanced the efficiency of H₂O₂ production by substituting the iridium catalyst with the molecular complex [CoIII(Cp*)(bpy)(H₂O)]2+ in the presence of Sc(NO₃)₃. Under optimized conditions, the system achieved a quantum yield of 37% at 450 nm and a solar-to-chemical energy conversion efficiency of 0.25%.
humanized_abstract: Solar generation of hydrogen peroxide as a clean energy vector using a photoredox couple of RuII(Me2phen)3 (2) and Ir(OH)3 in acidic water. Hydrogen peroxide (H2O2) was generated by solar-assisted photocatalytic reduction of dioxygen (O2) with simultaneous water oxidation in acidic aqueous solution. H2O2 was synthesized in O2-saturated sulfuric acid solutions in the presence of a photoluminescent RuII complex [RuII(Me2phen)3]2+ (2) as the photocatalyst and a wet powder of Ir(OH)3 as a water oxidation catalyst. Mechanistic studies using various spectroscopic tools demonstrated that H2O2 was generated through a process where the excited state of 2 oxidized O2 into a superoxide radical anion (O2˙) with the formation of a RuIII species [RuIII(Me2phen)3]3+. The resulting O2˙⁻ is subsequently protonated and subsequently undergoes disproportionation to form H2O2 as a stable product in competition with its back electron transfer to regenerate 2. The produced RuIII is converted back to RuII by water oxidation mediated by the Ir(OH)3, resulting in regeneration of the catalyst system. By substituting Ir(OH)3 with a molecular homogenous catalyst, the CoIII complex [CoIII(Cp*)(bpy)(H2O)]2+ with addition of Sc(NO3)3, a 37% quantum yield at 450 nm and solar-to-chemical energy conversion efficiency of 0.25% were achieved.
